file_insights.changes: Converted 3 stub endpoints into 4 fully functional schedule management endpoints (GET /calendar, POST /{id}/schedule, PUT /{id}/reschedule, GET /export/ical)
key_additions: Query parameter validation with FastAPI Query for start, end, scheduled_date, channel
HTTPException error handling for ValueError conditions
Response wrapper for iCal export with proper media type and disposition headers

file_insights.changes: New service layer implementation with 4 business logic methods for schedule operations
key_features: Async SQLAlchemy ORM queries with proper filtering and ordering
Content item status validation before scheduling (must be VALIDATED or SCHEDULED)
iCal generation using icalendar library with proper calendar metadata
Conditional channel filtering in reschedule operation

patterns_discovered.description: Business logic abstracted into ScheduleService class, separated from API routing layer
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reusability, testability, and separation of concerns

patterns_discovered.description: All database operations use async SQLAlchemy with await syntax
patterns_discovered.benefit: Non-blocking I/O, scalable concurrent request handling

patterns_discovered.description: AsyncSession passed through SessionDep parameter, ScheduleService instantiated per-request
icalendar library imported inside export_ical method rather than at module level
patterns_discovered.context: Defers import until needed, reduces startup overhead

patterns_discovered.description: SQLAlchemy select().where() chained syntax for dynamic query construction

gotchas_discovered.issue: N+1 Query Problem in iCal Export
gotchas_discovered.description: Iterates through entries and fetches ContentItem individually for each entry inside the loop. With 100 scheduled items, this creates 100+ database queries.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Performance degradation on large editorial calendars

gotchas_discovered.description: icalendar library imported inside method with no error handling if library is not installed
gotchas_discovered.impact: Runtime ImportError if icalendar not in requirements.txt

gotchas_discovered.issue: Reschedule Silently Fails on Non-PENDING Status
gotchas_discovered.description: reschedule() only finds entries with PENDING status. Other statuses raise generic 'not found' error without clarity on why
gotchas_discovered.impact: Confusing error messages for users attempting to reschedule already-published or cancelled items

gotchas_discovered.description: No validation that start < end. Reversed date ranges could cause unexpected query results
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential for client-side bugs or unexpected empty results

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses datetime.now(timezone.utc) assuming all system time is UTC, but no documentation of expected timezone for incoming datetime parameters
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential timezone mismatch if clients send naive datetimes

objectives_completed: ✓ Schedule service created with proper ORM integration
✓ Calendar date range query with optional channel filtering
✓ Schedule endpoint for new schedule entries with validation
✓ Reschedule endpoint with optional channel updates
✓ iCal export using icalendar library with proper formatting
✓ 4 API endpoints fully implemented and functional
approach_outcome.architecture_quality: Good - proper separation of concerns between API routes and service logic
approach_outcome.error_handling: Adequate - validates content status and catches ValueError with HTTP 400 responses
approach_outcome.code_coverage: All specified endpoints implemented with expected functionality

recommendations.recommendation: Fix N+1 query in export_ical() by joining ContentItem in the initial select or batch-fetching content items
recommendations.implementation: Use joinedload() or implement batch load: entries_dict = {e.id: e for e in entries}; content_ids = [e.content_id for e in entries]; items = await session.execute(select(ContentItem).where(ContentItem.id.in_(content_ids)))

recommendations.recommendation: Add icalendar to requirements.txt and move import to module level with clear dependency documentation
recommendations.implementation: Add 'icalendar>=5.0.0' to requirements.txt and import at top of schedule_service.py

recommendations.recommendation: Add start < end validation for date range endpoints
recommendations.implementation: Add validation in get_calendar() and export_ical(): if start and end and start >= end: raise HTTPException(400, 'start must be before end')

recommendations.recommendation: Improve error message for reschedule when entry is not in PENDING status
recommendations.implementation: Query without status filter first, check actual status, and provide specific error message about why rescheduling isn't allowed

recommendations.recommendation: Document timezone expectations for API datetime parameters
recommendations.implementation: Add docstring notes in API endpoints and service methods clarifying that datetimes should be ISO 8601 with timezone info

recommendations.recommendation: Consider implementing batch schedule/reschedule endpoints for bulk operations
recommendations.implementation: Add POST /batch/schedule and PUT /batch/reschedule endpoints accepting lists of scheduling operations

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding caching for iCal exports with cache invalidation on schedule changes
recommendations.implementation: Use Redis cache with cache key based on date range, invalidate on schedule/reschedule operations
